prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde (14 atoms) computed at DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, using PySCF RKS via the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill. Finite-difference Mulliken charges across the N, N+1 (anion, doublet), and N−1 (cation, doublet) states. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). All three SCFs converged; integrity status = 'ok', trustworthy = true. No warnings reported. The headline value reported is the highest f+ (most electrophilic site): Atom 3 (carbonyl carbon C), f+ = 0.39938967. The most nucleophilic site is Atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen O), f- = 0.23839906. Full per-atom f+/f-/f0/dual-descriptor values are tabulated above. Plot written to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-051024_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. No warnings were emitted (empty warnings array).
